description_long: This sand patch consists of a flat, irregularly shaped expanse of light brown sand with subtle, scattered patches of slightly lighter and darker tones, suggesting natural variation in the material. One edge of the patch features a raised, angular section, creating a small embankment or mound that casts a shadow onto the main surface. The surface is mostly smooth but dotted with faint, rounded spots that mimic the appearance of compacted or slightly damp sand. The boundaries of the patch are uneven and jagged, with one side showing a pronounced cut or break, as if the sand has been partially eroded or displaced. The overall appearance is dry and compact, with no visible vegetation or foreign objects embedded in the sand. This patch would be typical in urban environments where sand accumulates temporarily, such as near construction sites, roadside repairs, or playgrounds.
description: A flat, irregular sand patch with a raised embankment and scattered lighter spots, resembling compacted urban sand. The edges are jagged and uneven, with a dry, natural appearance.
description_view_0: From this view, the sand patch appears mostly flat with a visible raised embankment on the right side and a smooth, spotty surface.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the pronounced cut and shadowed embankment on the right, emphasizing the irregular boundary and depth.
description_view_2: The embankment is now on the left, showing the steepest edge and the shadowed undercut, with the flat surface extending to the right.
description_view_3: The raised embankment is visible on the left, with the rest of the patch appearing flat and the surface texture clearly visible.
attribute_location: urban construction site or roadside area
attribute_boundary_definition: The boundaries are irregular and jagged, with some edges appearing sharply defined and others blending more gradually into the surrounding surface.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: The sand patch appears partially blended with its surroundings, with some edges merging into adjacent surfaces and others standing out more distinctly.
